title: Broadcom Symantec SEP Tamper Protection Disable and Bypass Events
dataSources: `sourcetype=symantec:ep:security:file` SEP client security events including tamper and self-protection
app: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection (Splunkbase 2772)
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:security:file" earliest=-24h
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval desc=coalesce(Event_Description, event_description, Message, message, _raw)
| where match(lower(desc), "(?i)tamper|self.protect|protection disabled|disable tamper|bypass")
| eval user=coalesce(User_Name, user_name, "")
| stats count values(desc) as samples by host user
| sort -count
description: Tamper Protection prevents users or malware from shutting down SEP services. Alerts on disable or bypass attempts are strong indicators of insider misuse, compromised admin credentials, or malware trying to blind the agent.
value: Preserves integrity of endpoint controls and accelerates incident response when someone tries to weaken Symantec Endpoint Protection on critical systems.
implementation: (1) Forward SEP security / system events to Splunk with TA field extractions. (2) Tune strings for your SEPM locale. (3) Correlate with privileged OS logons on the same host. (4) Map to change tickets for expected maintenance. (5) Escalate uncorrelated events to IR.
visualization: Table (host, user, count, sample text), timeline, single-value (events last 24h).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection
Manage Tamper Protection in Symantec Endpoint Protection

grandmaExplanation: We alert when Broadcom Symantec’s self-protection is turned off or tampered with, because that often means an attacker is trying to disable antivirus.
